description: A bright golden rhizome from the ginger family, essential to Indian cooking. Turmeric is prized for its warm, earthy flavor and powerful medicinal properties rooted in Ayurvedic tradition.
flavorProfile: Earthy, slightly bitter, warm, mildly peppery with subtle sweetness

nutritionalProperties.note: Primary bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory properties

medicinalProperties.description: Curcumin in turmeric is a potent anti-inflammatory agent that inhibits NF-κB, a key inflammatory pathway. Used in Ayurveda for joint pain, arthritis, and general inflammation.
sources: Aggarwal BB et al., Biochem Pharmacol. 2007

medicinalProperties.description: Turmeric neutralizes free radicals and boosts the body's own antioxidant enzymes like superoxide dismutase and catalase.
Stimulates bile production and aids fat digestion. Traditional remedy for bloating, gas, and indigestion. Helps strengthen the digestive fire (Agni in Ayurveda).
May support cognitive function and potentially help prevent neurodegenerative diseases through its anti-inflammatory action.
Helps liver detoxification and protects against oxidative stress in the liver.

commonSubstitutes: Annatto powder (for color in vegetarian dishes)
Ginger (for warmth and flavor, but not color)
storageTips: Store ground turmeric in an airtight container away from direct sunlight and heat. Keep in a cool, dark place. Fresh turmeric rhizomes can be refrigerated in a plastic bag for up to 3 weeks. Ground turmeric maintains potency for 6-12 months.
buyingTips: Prefer organic turmeric for higher curcumin content. Look for bright, consistent golden color. Ceylon turmeric is considered premium. Avoid adulterated bright-orange powders which may contain lead oxide or other fillers. Buy whole rhizomes when possible and grind fresh for maximum potency.
